Florencia 13.description: American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles, California, composed mainly of Mexican Americans. Named after the Florence area of Los Angeles County and controlled by the Mexican Mafia. Involved in drug smuggling, murder, assault, robbery, and other crimes. Active since the 1930s with an estimated 2,000-3,000+ members.

Supreme Eiye Confraternity.description: The Supreme Eiye Confraternity (SEC) is a Nigerian confraternity founded in 1965 at the University of Ibadan. Originally a student group, it has evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, passport forgery, money laundering, and sex trafficking. It operates internationally with cells called 'forums' and is outlawed under Nigerian law.

descriptions: Black Axe and Supreme Eiye Confraternity engaged in violent fights resulting in casualties.
edges.evidence_quote: At least 53 people were injured or killed between March 2009 and June 2013 in fights between the Black Axe and their rivals, the Eiye confraternity.

descriptions: The Supreme Eiye Confraternity has a long-standing rivalry with Black Axe, involving killings of rival members.
edges.evidence_quote: The confraternity has a long-standing rivalry with Black Axe, another Nigerian confraternity-affiliated group.

descriptions: The Atlantic Guards were allied with the True Blue Americans.

Ichiwa-kai.description: The Ichiwa-kai was a yakuza gang based in Osaka, Japan, formed on June 13, 1984 by Hiroshi Yamamoto after splitting from the Yamaguchi-gumi. It had over 10,000 members and engaged in a violent war with the Yamaguchi-gumi known as the Yama-Ichi War. The organization dissolved in March 1989.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi fought a violent war.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing of the Yamaguchi Godfather sparked a Kansai-wide yakuza war between the two groups in which over 20 gangsters were killed.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai is a splinter group from Yamaguchi-gumi, and they were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Masahisa Takenaka was gunned down in 1985 by the Ichiwa-kai gang, a Yamaguchi Gumi splinter gang.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai was formed by a splinter from Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: It was formed on June 13, 1984 when Hiroshi Yamamoto, a top lieutenant in the Yamaguchi-gumi, broke from that gang to form his own organization with over 10,000 members.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai seceded from Yamaguchi-gumi and assassinated its boss, sparking the Yama–Ichi War.
edges.evidence_quote: However, Takenaka was assassinated at a girlfriend's home in Osaka early the following year by a rival group, the Ichiwa-kai , led by Hiroshi Yamamoto, who had seceded from the clan.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi fought Ichiwa-kai in the Yama-Ichi War.
edges.evidence_quote: Yama-Ichi War (1984–1989, against the Ichiwa-kai)

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai broke away and fought a war with Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Hiroshi Yamamoto broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi with many of its powerful members to form the Ichiwa-kai. A bitter rivalry existed between the two groups, which led to an all-out war (the Yama-Ichi War)

descriptions: UAB is allied with Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi

Puccio family.description: The Puccio family was an Argentine criminal family active in the early 1980s, based in San Isidro, Buenos Aires. The family, led by father Arquímedes Puccio, was convicted of four kidnappings and three murders. They targeted wealthy individuals for ransom, often killing victims despite payment. The family was arrested in 1985 after a police raid freed their last victim.

descriptions: The Maesano clan and the affiliated Puccio clan were involved together in money laundering.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2011 un'operazione condotta da Carabinieri e Guardia di Finanza ha portato al sequestro di beni mobili ed immobili per un valore di 4 milioni di euro frutto delle attività criminali e di riciclaggio svolte dal clan e dall'affiliato clan Puccio.

Fresno Bulldogs.description: Primarily Mexican American criminal street and prison gang based in Fresno, California, founded in 1984. One of the biggest drug gangs in Central California with an estimated 12,000 members. Engaged in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes. Unaffiliated with Sureños or Norteños, and rivals with multiple prison gangs.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1986 the F-14ers went to war with the Norteños, which led to a violent war in the California prison system known in gang folklore as "The Red Wave".

'Ndrina Mazzagatti.description: The Mazzagatti are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Polimeni and Bonarrigo clans and were involved in a feud with the Ferraro-Raccosta clans from 1992 to 1998 and again from 2011. The clan has been active in the cement business and extortion, with assets seized worth €120 million in 2007.

descriptions: Ferraro (as part of Ferraro-Raccosta) is in conflict with Mazzagatti.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1992 scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Oppido Mamertina tra i Ferraro-Raccosta e i Mazzagatti - Polimeni-Bonarrigo

descriptions: Mammoliti sided against Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011 sembra riesplosa la faida tra le due consorterie e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Mammoliti sided with Ferraro-Raccosta against the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011, la battaglia tra le due consorterie sembra riesplosa, e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Mazzagatti and Bonarrigo are allied in the conflict against Ferraro-Raccosta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana and NAR had converging interests, exemplified by a joint attempt to mislead the Bologna massacre investigation.
edges.evidence_quote: La probabile convergenza d'interessi tra gli uomini della Magliana, gli ambienti dell'eversione nera e alcuni settori deviati dei servizi e della politica, trova perfetta esemplificazione nel tentativo di depistaggio legato alla strage alla stazione ferroviaria di Bologna del 2 agosto 1980, per la quale vennero riconosciuti esecutori materiali (tra gli altri) alcuni militanti dei Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari

descriptions: Massimo Carminati, an ex-NAR member, was close to Banda della Magliana since the late 1970s.

Black Disciples.description: The Black Disciples is an African American street gang founded in Chicago, Illinois in 1966 by David Barksdale. Originally formed as the Devil's Disciples, it later merged with the Gangster Disciples to form the Black Gangster Disciples Nation before splitting again. The gang is involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering, and remains active with thousands of members across multiple US states.

descriptions: Black Disciples are listed as rivals of BPSN in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Black Disciples , Crips

descriptions: Infobox lists Black P. Stones as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Black Gangsters Disciples split from Black Disciples as a rival faction after Barksdale's death.
edges.evidence_quote: After Barksdale's death, ideological differences led to the creation of two rival distinct factions: the "Black Gangsters Disciples" and the "Black Disciples".

descriptions: Infobox lists Gangster Disciples as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Black Disciples merged with Gangster Disciples in 1969 to form Black Gangster Disciples Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1969, Larry Hoover , the leader of the rival gang Gangster Disciples , agreed to a merger with Barksdale to create a unified gang called the "Black Gangster Disciples Nation."

descriptions: Black Disciples was a constituent part of the merged Black Gangster Disciples Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1969, Larry Hoover , the leader of the rival gang Gangster Disciples , agreed to a merger with Barksdale to create a unified gang called the "Black Gangster Disciples Nation."

descriptions: Black Disciples splintered from BGDN and then engaged in conflict with Gangster Disciples, including shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Black Disciples decided to splinter from the Black Gangster Disciples, resulting in the reinvention of the original gang name and the incorporation of the new Gangster Disciples. Other members who felt disrespected by Hoover's declining orders decided to get his attention again by instigating gang-related shootings toward the new GD's.

descriptions: The two gangs were at war before merging.
edges.evidence_quote: For years, the Supreme Gangsters had an outstanding war with the Black Disciples Nation, led by David Barksdale.

descriptions: The two gangs agreed to a ceasefire and merged to form BGDN.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1969, Hoover and Barksdale agreed to a ceasefire. This resulted in the creation of the Black Gangster Disciple Nation.

March 23 Movement.description: The March 23 Movement (M23) is a Congolese rebel paramilitary group backed by Rwanda, based in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo. It was founded in 2012 by former members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) who mutinied against the government. M23 captured Goma in 2012 but withdrew after negotiations, and resumed operations in 2022. It is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance.

edges.target: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
descriptions: M23 opposes the FDLR.
edges.evidence_quote: The M23 is made up primarily of Tutsis and opposes the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR)

descriptions: M27 is composed of M23 defectors, indicating a splinter relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, Bertrand Bisimwa emerged as one of the leaders of the new rebel group called M27, which is composed of members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) and M23 defectors.

descriptions: Mai-Mai Cheka was an allied militia of M23.
edges.evidence_quote: the M23 relied on allied militias, including Nyatura, Mai-Mai Cheka

descriptions: A FARDC defector joined Mai-Mai PARECO, which was allied with M23.
edges.evidence_quote: Colonel Albert Kahasha, also called Foka Mike, deputy commander of the 808th FARDC Regiment, who joined the Mai-Mai PARECO group under La Fontaine in Bunyatenge, south of Lubero, in June 2012.

edges.target: National Congress for the Defence of the People

edges.evidence_quote: Led by Tahanga Nyolo and allied with M23, this faction established a foothold in the Rwenzori region.

descriptions: M23 lists Wagner Group as an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Wagner Group (allegedly), Wazalendo, Burundi, South Africa, Malawi, Tanzania, MONUSCO, FDLR

Gang des postiches.description: A team of bank robbers that operated in Paris between 1981 and 1986, robbing around thirty banks. They wore false mustaches, beards, and wigs to disguise themselves. The gang was known for being respectful to hostages and gained public sympathy. Their total theft may have exceeded 30 million euros.

descriptions: The methods of the Gang des Tractions Avant were continued by the Gang des Postiches.
edges.evidence_quote: were mostly continued by a number of other gangs, notably the gang des postiches

descriptions: Black Jackets and 210 International are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rock Machine , Veles gang , Black Dogs , Bandogs , 210 International , Dogpack 416 , Black Army , Holmblaaa , Kommando 210

descriptions: Firehouse Crew is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: The Partinico faction is a sub-group within the Detroit Partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: The 'Partinico faction' was a group of Detroit Mafia leaders that included Raffaele Quasarano and deported Sicilian-American mafiosi, Francesco 'Frankie Fingers' Coppola.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham jointly participated in attacks with Jesus Son of Mary Battalions.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham along with other Free Syrian Army-aligned factions including the Jesus Son of Mary Battalions and Alwiya al-Furqan jointly took part in the ISIL-led attacks code named 'Volcano of Rage'

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham participated in operations as part of National Front for Liberation.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham participated in the Operation Deterrence of Aggression which began on November 27, 2024, as part of the National Front for Liberation.

descriptions: Jaysh al-Ahrar joined the National Front for Liberation with Ahrar al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: Jaysh al-Ahrar (Left HTS in mid-2017 due to disagreements with the leadership becoming an independent faction, later joined the National Front for Liberation with Ahrar al-Sham in August 2018)

Solid Wood Soldiers.description: Neo-Nazi prison gang and organized crime syndicate based in Texas, founded in 1990 at Snake River Correctional Institution in Oregon. Known for drug trafficking, assault, murder, and racketeering, with strong ties to other white supremacist groups. Uses distinctive tattoos and symbols, including the number four representing honor, courage, respect, and loyalty.

Greenwich Village Crew.description: The Greenwich Village Crew is a crew within the Genovese crime family, active in the Greenwich Village area of Manhattan. Founded in the 1920s by Vito Genovese, it has been involved in racketeering, gambling, loan sharking, and extortion. The crew was historically led by Vincent Gigante, who became boss of the Genovese family. It remains active, though its current leadership is uncertain.

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is a crew operating under the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Greenwich Village Crew operates in Greenwich Village in Lower Manhattan.

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Bonanno crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Colombo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is a sub-unit of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Greenwich Village Crew is a crew within the Genovese crime family

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

King Cobras.description: The King Cobras are a prominent Pacific gang in New Zealand, founded by Samoans in Ponsonby in the 1950s, making them the oldest gang in the country. Initially restricted to Samoans, they have since accepted other Polynesians. The gang is known for violence, firearms possession, and involvement in drug dealing, including methamphetamine, cocaine, and marijuana. They have been involved in major drug cases and murders.

descriptions: Infobox lists King Cobras as an ally of Black Power.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Crips , Killa Beez (gang) , King Cobras (gang) New Zealand Nomads

descriptions: Head Hunters killed a member of King Cobras in 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: convicted of murdering a member of the King Cobras, Siaosi Evalu

descriptions: King Cobras are allied with Black Power according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bloods , Crips , Black Power , Central Auckland street gangs

descriptions: King Cobras are allied with Bloods according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bloods , Crips , Black Power , Central Auckland street gangs

descriptions: King Cobras are allied with Crips according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bloods , Crips , Black Power , Central Auckland street gangs

descriptions: King Cobras and Head Hunters cooperated in a large methamphetamine ring in 2003.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, members of the King Cobras ran a large methamphetamine ring along with the Head Hunters in one of Aucklands biggest drug cases.

descriptions: Four Corner Hustlers are rivals of New Breed Black Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Black Souls , New Breed Black Gangsters , Mafia Insane Vice Lords , Angelo Four Corner Hustlers

Siderno Group.description: The Siderno Group is a criminal association related to the 'Ndrangheta, based in Siderno, Calabria, Italy. It operates in Canada, Australia, the United States, and Europe, primarily involved in global cocaine trafficking and money laundering. The group was formed by migrants from Siderno who moved to Canada and Australia in the 1950s. It maintains close ties with the Commisso 'ndrina and other families.

descriptions: Siderno Group is a part of 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Commisso was related to Cosimo 'The Quail' Commisso of Siderno, Italy, considered by police there to be an 'Ndrangheta organized crime boss' of the Siderno Group

descriptions: The Siderno Group is a consortium of families from Siderno, which is part of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni cinquanta proprio in Canada e Australia nasce il cosiddetto Siderno Group , un consorzio di famiglie originarie di Siderno con collegamenti anche con elementi di Cosa nostra statunitense come Frank Costello dediti al traffico di eroina e di armi.

descriptions: Infobox lists Siderno Group as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Musitano crime family, Rizzuto crime family, Siderno Group, and various other gangs in the Buffalo area

descriptions: The Canadian Camera di Controllo coordinates activities and resolves disputes for the Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: A Camera di Controllo also exists in Canada, according to 'confidential information' of the police 'which they believe to be reliable.' It consists of six or seven Toronto-area men, who co-ordinate activities and resolves disputes among the Siderno Group in Southern Ontario.

descriptions: The Commisso 'ndrina's Canadian branch is part of the Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: The 'ndrina is based in Siderno , but also has a branch in the Greater Toronto Area in Canada as part of the Siderno Group .

descriptions: Commisso is part of the Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono anche tra coloro che le forze dell'ordine canadesi in sinergia con le forze dell'ordine italiane hanno ribattezzato Siderno Group.

descriptions: The Musitano family had a cocaine trade agreement with the Siderno Group, which was violated by Racco.
edges.evidence_quote: Tony orchestrated the murder of Toronto mobster Domenic Racco of the Siderno Group, who violated their cocaine trade agreement by dealing behind Musitano's back

descriptions: Infobox lists Siderno Group as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Siderno Group

descriptions: The Siderno Group is related to the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Siderno Group is a criminal association in Canada, Australia and Italy related to the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Siderno Group had connections with US Cosa Nostra members like Frank Costello for heroin and arms trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni cinquanta proprio in Canada e Australia nasce il cosiddetto Siderno Group , un consorzio di famiglie originarie di Siderno con collegamenti anche con elementi di Cosa nostra statunitense come Frank Costello dediti al traffico di eroina e di armi.

descriptions: The Coluccio clan is part of the Toronto-area Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toronto-area Siderno group has included the Coluccio ... crime families

descriptions: The Commisso 'ndrina is based in Siderno and is part of the Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Siderno is home to one of the 'Ndrangheta's biggest and most important clans, the Commisso 'ndrina

descriptions: Michele Racco, head of the Siderno Group, maintained close contacts with the Cotroni crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: He maintained close contacts with ... the Cotroni crime family in Montreal.

descriptions: The Figliomeni clan is part of the Toronto-area Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toronto-area Siderno group has included the ... Figliomeni ... crime families

descriptions: Michele Racco, head of the Siderno Group, maintained close contacts with the Luppino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: He maintained close contacts with the Luppino crime family in Hamilton, Ontario

descriptions: The Musitano family killed Domenic Racco of the Siderno Group due to a cocaine trade dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: Anthony Musitano of the Hamilton-based Musitano crime family ... orchestrated the murder of Domenic Racco, who violated their cocaine trade agreement

descriptions: Infobox lists Siderno Group as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Siderno Group

descriptions: The Wolfpack and Siderno Group were engaged in a gang war in Toronto from 2016 to 2018.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack became involved in a gang war in Toronto in 2016-2018 with the Siderno Group.

Sacra Corona Unita.description: Sacra Corona Unita is a Mafia-type criminal organization from the Apulia region in Southern Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Pino Rogoli. It is active in areas such as Brindisi, Lecce, and Taranto, and engages in cigarette smuggling, arms trafficking, drug trafficking, human trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and political corruption. The group was formed to counter the power of other mafia organizations in prisons and Apulian territory. After arrests and defections in the early 2000s, it no longer exists as a single viable organization.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta has stipulated various alliances with Sacra Corona Unita for business management.
edges.evidence_quote: ha stipulato nel corso della storia varie alleanze per la gestione degli affari

descriptions: The text states 'Ndrangheta has relationships with all Italian mafias, including Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto e ha rapporti con tutte le mafie italiane : Cosa nostra , Camorra , Banda della Magliana , Sacra Corona Unita , Basilischi .

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta authorized the founding of Sacra Corona Unita in 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: La Sacra Corona Unita (S.C.U.) si articola in una serie di doti o gradi , molto simili a quelli della 'ndrangheta , che infatti autorizzò la sua fondazione nel 1981

descriptions: Umberto Bellocco authorized the founding of the Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Il capobastone Umberto Bellocco conferì il grado di Santista a Giuseppe Rogoli , il quale, col suo permesso e quello di Carmine Alvaro fondò nel 1981 la Sacra Corona Unita

descriptions: Umberto Bellocco ordered the founding of the Sacra Corona Unita by Giuseppe Rogoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Rogoli , (fondatore della Sacra Corona Unita per ordine di Umberto Bellocco, arrestato, sta scontando 3 ergastoli nel carcere di Viterbo ).

descriptions: Bellocco helped found the SCU, a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: In the beginning of the 1980s Umberto Bellocco was involved in setting up the Sacra Corona Unita (SCU)

descriptions: Pelle cooperated with Sacra Corona Unita members in international drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: insieme ad elementi dei Fidanzati di Cosa Nostra e membri della Sacra Corona Unita pugliese

descriptions: American Mafia has links with Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: The American Mafia, in addition to having links with organized crime groups in Italy such as the Camorra, the 'Ndrangheta, Sacra Corona Unita and Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: The American Mafia lists Sacra Corona Unita as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , Sacra corona unita , varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita was originally founded as a subgroup of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata (Camorra).
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Raffaele Cutolo , entrusted Pino Iannelli and Alessandro Fusco the task of founding an organization in Apulia to be directly controlled by the Nuova Camorra Organizzata , the organization was named Nuova Camorra Pugliese, later called Sacra Corona Unita .

descriptions: Biancoli later joined Sacra Corona Unita and definitively left in 1986.
edges.evidence_quote: per poi passare alla Sacra Corona Unita di Giuseppe Rogoli, da cui si staccò definitivamente nel 1986

descriptions: Di Lauro clan has links to Sacra Corona Unita in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the pentito Antonio Accurso, the Di Lauro clan has several links to the Sacra Corona Unita in question with regards to drug trafficking.

descriptions: Di Lauro clan has ties to Sacra Corona Unita for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan also has ties to the Sacra Corona Unita and Ciro Contini, a member of the Contini Clan, regarding drug trafficking.

descriptions: Mercante dissociated from Sacra Corona Unita in 1986.
edges.evidence_quote: Anche Parisi e Mercante si dissociarono dalla Sacra Corona Unita nel 1986

descriptions: Gruppo Annacondia remained independent from Sacra Corona Unita, not joining it.
edges.evidence_quote: Come quello tarantino dei Modeo, ha avuto una certa indipendenza dalla Sacra Corona Unita: infatti Annacondia non aveva aderito alla SCU voluta da Rogoli, preferendo rimanere affiliato solo con Cosa nostra e con Alessandro Modeo

descriptions: NCO established strong ties with Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: The NCO also established strong ties with the Apulian Sacra Corona Unita

descriptions: NCO allied with Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: alleanze con altre realtà delinquenziali extra-regionali diventano numerose: oltre che con la Sacra Corona Unita pugliese

descriptions: Members of Nuova Camorra Pugliese later joined Sacra Corona Unita before splitting off.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel giro di pochi anni, Iannelli e Rizzi si liberarono da qualsiasi vincolo con la camorra cutoliana e aderirono alla Sacra Corona Unita , da cui però si staccarono nel 1986 a causa di contrasti con Giuseppe Rogoli , dando vita alla cosiddetta " Società foggiana " .

descriptions: The Nuova camorra pugliese was absorbed by the Sacra corona unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Il periodo di attività della Nuova camorra pugliese va dall'inizio degli anni '70 fino a metà degli anni '80, quando fu definitivamente assorbita dalla Sacra corona unita di Giuseppe Rogoli.

descriptions: NGCP was the precursor to Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: the Nuova Grande Camorra Pugliese, which was the precursor to the Sacra Corona Unita in Apulia

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia Salentina was formed after a peace agreement with Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: A seguito della "pace" stipulata da Pantaleo De Matteis (detto Pantaluccio ) e dal cognato Salvatore Rizzo con la SCU di Rogoli, nel 1986 nacque nel carcere di Lecce la Nuova Famiglia Salentina

descriptions: Parisi and Mercante dissociated from Sacra Corona Unita in 1986.
edges.evidence_quote: Anche Parisi e Mercante si dissociarono dalla Sacra Corona Unita nel 1986

descriptions: The Polverino clan supplied hashish to the Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Polverino, the leader of the Polverino clan before his arrest, was considered the 'hashish king', due to his monopoly on the importation of hashish from Morocco to Italy via Spain supplying not only all the Camorra clans, but also the 'Ndrangheta, the Sicilian Mafia and the Sacra Corona Unita.

descriptions: Polverino clan supplied hashish to Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Polverino, was dubbed the 'hashish king', due to his monopoly on the importation of hashish from Morocco to Italy via Spain supplying not only all the Camorra clans, but also the 'Ndrangheta, the Sicilian Mafia and the Sacra Corona Unita.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita suppressed Remo Lecce Libera, threatening and killing its members.
edges.evidence_quote: La Remo Lecce Libera richiedeva infatti l'indipendenza dei criminali leccesi da qualsiasi organizzazione criminale che non fosse la 'Ndrangheta ma venne stroncata sul nascere dalla Sacra Corona Unita , che minacciò pesantemente di uccidere i suoi affiliati, come infatti avvenne con l'omicidio di Donato Erpete nel 1989 .

descriptions: Infobox lists 'Ndrangheta as an ally of Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Società foggiana, Albanian mafia, Serbian mafia, Montenegrin mafia, Corsican mafia, Romanian Mafia

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita was founded with permission from 'Ndrangheta member Umberto Bellocco.
edges.evidence_quote: L'organizzazione della Sacra Corona Unita (SCU) è stata fondata nel 1981 da Giuseppe Rogoli all'interno del carcere di Bari , dove era detenuto, con il permesso di Umberto Bellocco (capo dell' omonima 'ndrina di Rosarno ) .

descriptions: The Sacra corona unita was founded with permission from an 'Ndrangheta member.
edges.evidence_quote: Sacra corona unita, fondata nel 1983 da Giuseppe Rogoli nel carcere di Bari con il permesso dello 'ndranghetista Umberto Bellocco

descriptions: Elements of Sacra Corona Unita may have originated from 'Ndrangheta or cooperated with it.
edges.evidence_quote: It has also been suggested that elements of this group originated from the 'Ndrangheta, but it is not known if they were breakaways from it or the result of indirect co-operation with clans of the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta created Sacra Corona Unita as a response to Cutolo's expansion.
edges.evidence_quote: Come risposta al tentativo di Cutolo di espandersi in Capitanata, la 'Ndrangheta diede vita alla Sacra Corona Unita (SCU), associazione malavitosa di stampo mafioso formata da esponenti locali.

descriptions: Bellocco 'ndrina boss endorsed SCU activities and conferred a dowry on an SCU member.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal activities of the criminal association would always have been endorsed by the 'Ndrangheta boss Umberto Bellocco who, after 21 years in prison, would have met the Sacra Corona Unita member Cataldo Caporosso and conferred on him the dowry of padrino.

descriptions: Giuseppe Rogoli was affiliated with the Bellocco clan and obtained permission to found the first Apulian 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: il mesagnese Giuseppe Rogoli, detto Pino, già affiliato al clan della 'ndrangheta Bellocco di Rosarno, chiesto e ottenuto il permesso al capobastone Umberto Bellocco, fondò la prima 'ndrina pugliese all'interno del carcere di Bari

descriptions: La Rosa is a branch (propagine) of SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Rogoli affidò a Oronzo Romano e Giovanni Dalena la costituzione di un'altra 'ndrina propagine della SCU nel sud barese chiamata La Rosa

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita collaborates with Italian-American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: These Italian crime groups frequently collaborate with the Italian-American Mafia, which is itself an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia.

descriptions: Infobox lists Camorra as an ally of Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Società foggiana, Albanian mafia, Serbian mafia, Montenegrin mafia, Corsican mafia, Romanian Mafia

descriptions: SCU incorporated Camorra traditions under Rogoli's leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: Under his leadership the SCU mixed its Apulian interests and opportunities with 'Ndrangheta and Camorra traditions.

descriptions: Camorra members were sent to Apulia, but no direct relationship with SCU is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: A partire dagli anni Cinquanta, la Puglia, come altre regioni d'Italia, fu scelta come zona dove inviare al soggiorno obbligato pericolosi criminali appartenenti a Cosa nostra, alla 'Ndrangheta e alla Camorra.

descriptions: The Brindisi feud targeted major SCU members and was led by the Buccarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: capeggiata allora dal clan Buccarella ( Tuturano ) .

descriptions: Di Lauro clan has links to SCU regarding drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: However, according to the pentito Antonio Accurso, the Camorra's Di Lauro clan has several links to the Sacra Corona Unita in question with regards to drug trafficking.

descriptions: Famiglia Salentina Libera was formed in response to SCU's formation.
edges.evidence_quote: In risposta alla formazione della SCU, nacque nel carcere di Pianosa la Famiglia Salentina Libera (F.S.L.)

descriptions: SCU was created to counter the power of Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sacra Corona Unita was created to counter the overwhelming power in the prisons and on the Apulian territory of some of the more established mafia organisations, such as the Campania families united in Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita was founded in response to Nuova Camorra Organizzata's expansion attempts.
edges.evidence_quote: Nasce in risposta al tentativo della Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Raffaele Cutolo di espandersi in Puglia .

descriptions: SCU was created in response to NCO's expansion attempt.
edges.evidence_quote: Come risposta al tentativo di Cutolo di espandersi in Capitanata, la 'Ndrangheta diede vita alla Sacra Corona Unita (SCU)

descriptions: Sacra Corona Unita was originally the Nuova Grande Camorra Pugliese.
edges.evidence_quote: The SCU was founded in the late 1970s as the Nuova Grande Camorra Pugliese

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia Salentina fought a feud against SCU's clans in Lecce.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuova Famiglia Salentina : formed in 1986 by De Matteis Pantaleo, from Lecce and stemming from the Famiglia Salentina Libera born in the early 1980s as an autonomous criminal movement in the Salento area with no links with extra-regional Mafia expressions, the organization fought a feud against SCU's clans in Lecce;

descriptions: SCU made peace with Famiglia Salentina Libera, which later became Nuova Famiglia Salentina.
edges.evidence_quote: la pace siglata nel carcere di Lecce tra Pino Rogoli e la Famiglia Salentina Libera, guidata dal boss leccese Salvatore Rizzo e dal cognato Pantaleo De Matteis, che cambiò nome in Nuova Famiglia Salentina (NFL)

descriptions: Nuova Sacra Corona Unita was a refounded version of Sacra Corona Unita after a split.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1986 il gruppo criminale barese capeggiato da Savinuccio Parisi e quello foggiano di Giosuè Rizzi e Pino Iannelli, risentiti con Pino Rogoli a causa delle sue esplicite ammissioni nel processo in cui erano imputati insieme a Bari, si staccarono dalla Sacra corona unita e divennero autonomi. Iniziò la rifondazione della Sacra corona unita partendo dalle modalità di affiliazione, con regole più rigide e severe. Così nel carcere di Trani nacque la Nuova Sacra corona unita (NSCU)

descriptions: Remo Lecce Libera is an antagonistic group independent from SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Remo Lecce Libera : formed by some leading criminal figures from Lecce, who claim to be independent from any criminal group other than the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Rosa dei Venti split from SCU due to conflicts with Rogoli, with permission from 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La Rosa dei venti prese vita nel carcere di Lecce nel 1990 da Giovanni De Tommasi, Cosimo Cirfeta e Vincenzo Stranieri che richiesero il permesso alla 'Ndrangheta di staccarsi dalla Sacra Corona Unita per contrasti avuti con Rogoli

descriptions: Rosa dei Venti formed from an internal division in SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Rosa dei Venti : formed in 1990 by De Tommasi in the Lecce prison, following an internal division in the SCU.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera was founded by former SCU members after conflicts with SCU leaders.
edges.evidence_quote: La Sacra Corona Libera fu fondata nel 1998 da Massimo D'Amico, Antonio Vitale e Massimo Pasimeni, criminali mesagnesi già appartenuti alla Sacra corona unita.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera was founded by some members of SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Alcuni di loro hanno fondato la Sacra corona libera.

descriptions: Società foggiana emerged after the murder of an SCU lieutenant, indicating a split or rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The murder of Giuseppe Laviano, lieutenant of the Sacra Corona Unita in Foggia (whose body was never found), in January 1989, marked a turning point in the mafia war and the rise of Rocco Moretti, known as il porco (the pig), in the context of the Foggia organized crime at the expense of Laviano himself. After the murder, Moretti became the head of the new criminal organization Società foggiana in Foggia.

descriptions: Società foggiana split from Sacra Corona Unita in 1986 due to conflicts.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel giro di pochi anni, Iannelli e Rizzi si liberarono da qualsiasi vincolo con la camorra cutoliana e aderirono alla Sacra Corona Unita , da cui però si staccarono nel 1986 a causa di contrasti con Giuseppe Rogoli , dando vita alla cosiddetta " Società foggiana " .

descriptions: Infobox lists Società foggiana as an ally of Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Società foggiana, Albanian mafia, Serbian mafia, Montenegrin mafia, Corsican mafia, Romanian Mafia

descriptions: Società foggiana is an offshoot of Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: Società foggiana , an offshoot of Sacra Corona Unita, in Apulia

descriptions: Società foggiana is a mafia cartel linked to Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: La Società foggiana è un cartello criminale di stampo mafioso, legato alla Sacra corona unita

descriptions: The Società foggiana is independent from Sacra Corona Unita, not a part of it.
edges.evidence_quote: These were wrongly reported as the work of the Sacra Corona Unita (the fourth mafia) by news media, unaware of the new independent mafia in Foggia province. "But that wasn't the case. We are witnessing what should be called a fifth mafia, independent of the Sacra Corona Unita" according to Giuseppe Volpe, a prosecutor and anti-mafia head of Bari.

descriptions: Società Foggiana split from Sacra Corona Unita.
edges.evidence_quote: from which the Società Foggiana split

descriptions: Butler Gents was a constituent gang that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.
